commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ad4b37b3507d3d8ab4b83f438810cec21883af61)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds memory-core cron pressure checks, a rotating two-workspace managed dreaming batch, heartbeat-as-cron handling, and focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection shows current main loops over every resolved dreaming workspace for a cron event without a pressure guard, and the linked report provides redacted logs of a 10-workspace tick under pressure. I did not run a live gateway or tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/maintainer follow-up for real behavior proof, documentation of cadence semantics, and explicit acceptance of the fixed batch policy under the protected maintainer label.

target.title: fix(memory-core): bound managed dreaming under pressure
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: managed memory dreaming cron could sweep every configured workspace in one gateway tick, including while the process was already under memory pressure.
- Solution: bound cron-triggered managed dreaming to a rotating two-workspace batch and defer cron work when RSS or heap warning thresholds are exceeded.
- What changed: queued managed heartbeat dreaming events are treated as cron work, pressure-deferred work is retried because the workspace cursor advances only after attempted work, and focused memory-core regressions cover the cron paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): direct non-cron dreaming requests are not batch-limited, and this does not change memory storage formats or plugin configuration.

## Motivation

- Observed gateway performance logs showed managed memory dreaming processing 10 workspaces in one background tick, taking 69621 ms, while nearby diagnostics reported high RSS and saturated event-loop/CPU conditions.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84847
- Closes #84849
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Managed memory-core dreaming cron is bounded and deferred under gateway RSS/heap pressure instead of sweeping every workspace in one tick.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree on Node 24.15.0 with focused memory-core Vitest coverage for the managed cron seam; before evidence comes from redacted runtime diagnostics captured from the affected gateway.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm format:fix extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```shell
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts
[test] passed extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts in 6.96s
1 file passed, 51 tests passed

$ git diff --check
# no output

$ AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```

- Observed result after fix: The regression tests now verify that managed cron dreaming handles at most 2 workspaces per tick, rotates to deferred work on the next cron tick, does not advance past work skipped by mid-batch memory pressure, and defers queued managed heartbeat cron events under pressure.
- What was not tested: A live multi-workspace gateway run with private agent/session data was not rerun; the fix is verified at the memory-core cron seam with redacted before evidence and focused tests. The broader checkpoint artifact contributor is covered separately by #84824.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```shell
memory-core: dreaming promotion complete (workspaces=10, candidates=10, applied=1, failed=0)
observed managed background task duration: 69621ms
memory pressure warning: rssBytes=1827979264 heapUsedBytes=684072312 thresholdBytes=1610612736
liveness warning: eventLoopUtilization=0.991 cpuCoreRatio=1.107 active=0 waiting=0 queued=0
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Managed cron dreaming collected every configured workspace and processed them in one cron delivery without a per-tick batch bound or a process memory pressure guard.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered short-term dreaming promotion but did not exercise multi-workspace managed cron batching, pressure deferral, or queued managed heartbeat cron delivery.
- Contributing context (if known): The managed heartbeat path can deliver the cron system event through the before-agent-reply hook, so it needs to enter the same cron guard path as a direct cron trigger.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: managed cron dreaming batches workspaces, rotates deferred work, defers under RSS/heap pressure, and treats queued managed heartbeat dreaming events as cron work for pressure checks.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is owned inside the memory-core dreaming cron seam and can be exercised without private gateway sessions or provider credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing dreaming tests covered promotion behavior but not the pressure and batching cases.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Background memory dreaming may take multiple cron ticks to sweep all workspaces when many workspaces are configured, and cron-triggered dreaming is skipped while gateway RSS or heap usage is above the warning threshold.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
cron tick -> all managed workspaces -> memory promotion and narratives in one gateway tick

After:
cron tick -> pressure check -> two-workspace batch -> cursor advances after attempted work -> deferred work runs on later ticks
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux worktree
- Runtime/container: Node 24.15.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): memory-core plugin cron seam
- Relevant config (redacted): memory-core dreaming enabled with multiple agent workspaces

### Steps

1. Run the focused memory-core dreaming tests.
2. Exercise cron-triggered dreaming with multiple workspaces and pressure-mocked process memory.
3. Confirm deferred work is retried and queued heartbeat delivery enters the cron pressure path.

### Expected

- Cron-triggered managed dreaming does bounded work per tick and defers under RSS/heap pressure.

### Actual

- After the patch, focused tests pass for batching, cursor advancement, and pressure deferral.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [x] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: format, focused memory-core dreaming Vitest coverage, diff hygiene, and autoreview.
- Edge cases checked: queued managed heartbeat delivery under pressure and mid-batch memory pressure before the second workspace.
- What you did **not** verify: private live multi-workspace gateway run.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Large managed memory setups may take more cron ticks to fully promote memories.
  - Mitigation: The cursor rotates across workspaces so deferred work is attempted on later ticks, while direct non-cron promotion remains unbounded by this cron batch guard.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
